name: Catholic & Community
description_short: City & County Credit Union (CCCU) is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union offering checking, savings, mortgages, auto loans, credit cards, and personal loans with competitive rates and no hidden fees.
description_long:
City & County Credit Union operates as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution focused on serving its community through accessible financial services. The organization emphasizes its member-centric model, positioning itself in contrast to for-profit banks by reinvesting profits back to members rather than shareholders. CCCU maintains a physical branch and ATM network alongside digital banking services.

CCCU offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including free checking accounts, savings accounts, 9-month CDs, credit cards, auto loans (advertised from 4.49% APR), mortgages (30-year fixed at 6.442% APR), personal loans, and business solutions. The credit union also provides financial education resources including workshops, credit report reviews, budgeting guides, and a blog called "The Pink Pages" with money management tips. Current promotions include up to $1,000 off mortgage closing costs.

The institution distinguishes itself through its member-ownership model, emphasizing that members have a voice in organizational direction and benefit from lower fees and better rates compared to traditional banks. CCCU positions financial education and transparency as core differentiators, offering free resources like "The Ultimate Guide to Financial Wellness" and "Your Step-By-Step Guide to Switching Financial Institutions." The marketing emphasizes people-focused service over sales quotas and profit extraction.

As a credit union, CCCU is regulated by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration) and operates under a cooperative structure. The main limitation for consumers is membership eligibility—credit unions typically restrict service to specific geographic areas or employee groups, though this is not explicitly detailed on the provided website content. Rates and terms are competitive but should be compared with local banking alternatives for individual needs.
services: Free checking accounts with no monthly fees
Savings accounts with flexible savings options
Auto loans with rates from 4.49% APR
Mortgages for home purchase with closing cost assistance
Credit cards with rewards and no confusing fine print
Business solutions and small business financing
Free credit report reviews with CCCU team members
Financial wellness workshops and classes (online and in-person)
Mortgage pre-approval and home buying guidance
pros: Offers free checking with no account fees and straightforward features
Competitive auto loan rates advertised as low as 4.49% APR with quick approvals
Mortgage closing cost promotion up to $1,000 off for home purchases
Free financial education including workshops, credit report reviews, and budgeting guides
9-month CD rates up to 3.80% APY competitive with market offerings
Member-ownership structure means profits return to members through better rates and lower fees
Comprehensive product suite including mortgages, auto loans, personal loans, business solutions, and credit cards in one institution
cons: Credit union membership typically requires eligibility criteria (geographic location or employment) not clearly disclosed on website
Limited information about personal loan terms, rates, and maximum loan amounts on the website
No details provided about business loan terms, rates, or eligibility requirements despite offering business solutions
Credit card APR starts at 11.50%, which is higher than many premium credit card products
Website does not specify NCUA insurance coverage limits or other regulatory protections explicitly
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking transparent mortgage options with closing cost assistance
Auto buyers prioritizing competitive fixed rates and quick loan approval processes
Credit union-eligible consumers in CCCU's service area seeking fee-free checking and comprehensive financial services
Savers looking for free financial education and guidance when managing personal finances
diagnosis: City & County Credit Union is best for individuals eligible for membership in its service area who want comprehensive banking services, competitive rates, and commitment to financial education without profit-driven sales pressure. The primary caveat is that credit union membership requires meeting specific eligibility requirements (typically geographic or employment-based) that may exclude some consumers from joining.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Catholic & Community — Shiloh, IL Credit Union | CreditDoc
meta_description: Catholic & Community — NCUA-insured credit union in Shiloh, IL. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
